body.title: Inference router: express rules as dimension->capability-envelope policy (no model/provider literals)
body.intention: I intend that a routing rule expresses policy over dimensions and emits a capability envelope, never naming a specific model or provider.
body.description:
Replace the rule SHAPE (stack 4 of 4) per D-inference-router-stack-decomposition-2026-07-08. Today RoutingRule hardcodes (task_class, model_id, source_name). A PolicyRule instead maps dimension predicates -> a capability envelope: min difficulty, required domain, required features, tier gating. It NEVER names a model_id or source_name.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/rules_engine.py (a PolicyRule dataclass + a default policy set, built ALONGSIDE the existing RoutingRule/_DEFAULT_RULES); tests/inference/test_rules_as_policy.py.
**Design rules:** inference-tier-not-model; docs-live-in-code; test-plan-or-why-not.
**Scope boundary:** add the PolicyRule shape + default policies ONLY, alongside the existing triples. Do NOT delete _DEFAULT_RULES or change route() (that is the cutover ticket). Additive.
**Test plan:** a PolicyRule narrows a RouteRequest's dimensions to a capability envelope; a `git grep` over the new default policy set finds NO model_id or source_name literal (the anti-triple invariant, at the rules layer). Red before PolicyRule exists -> green after.
**Rollback:** remove PolicyRule + default policies + test; the legacy triples still serve.
**Logging:** log policy-match (dims -> envelope) at DEBUG.
**Depends on:** T-inference-dimensions-contract (needs RouteRequest).
**Observability:** the no-literal grep assertion is the runtime-observable guarantee that rules carry no provider/model.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)

[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Added the rules-as-policy stack (stack 4 of 4). PolicyRule matches dimension predicates (domain/ticket_tier/builder_tier/urgency) and contributes to a CapabilityEnvelope (min_difficulty/required_domain/required_features) — NEVER a model/provider literal (tested invariant). build_envelope seeds from the request and monotonically tightens. Put in its own policy.py (deviation from ticket's rules_engine.py suggestion) so the rules stack is first-class like the other 3 and the monolith triples delete cleanly at cutover. ADDITIVE. Proof a6620843 red=AssertionError->green; 5/5 tests pass.
